name: expand
porting_notes: Single-file port (170 lines, pure C89). Transformations: err()/errx() via amiport/err.h shim, pledge() stubbed as macro, exit(1) changed to exit(10) (RETURN_ERROR), __progname defined directly to avoid weak-symbol linker stripping. Performance optimised: getchar() loop replaced with fgets() + pointer scan (~40x fewer JSR calls per line); putchar(' ') loops replaced with fwrite(spaces, 1, n, stdout) (~7x fewer calls per tab); modulo in single-stop case replaced with precomputed target (1 DIVU per tab vs 7). Memory audit: CLEAN -- single allocation (amiport_expand_argv), atexit cleanup covers all exit paths. 29/29 FS-UAE tests pass.
known_limitations: No multibyte/locale support -- tab expansion is byte-based, correct for ISO 8859-1.
readme:
Short:        Convert tabs to spaces
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       University of California, Berkeley (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.15

expand - Convert tabs to spaces

Original author: University of California, Berkeley
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in vamos emulator and FS-UAE.

Usage:
  expand [-t tablist] [file ...]

  expand file.txt           Expand tabs to spaces (8-column stops)
  expand -t 4 file.txt      Use 4-column tab stops
  expand -t 4,8,12 file.txt Custom tab stops at columns 4, 8, 12

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
